name: Logistics Battalion
short_description: Take on the role as a WW2 supply manager from 1939 to 1940 and make sure the troops in your sector get what they need to push the enemy back. Choose between food, ammunition and fuel, who needs what and how much. The success of the troops largely depend on you supplying them!
detailed_description:
Logistics Battalion puts you in charge of logistics and managing to supply the troops with adequate supplies during 1939-1940. Manage the resources and the requests, make sure to deliver on time and choose between several different transport methods.

Gain advantages, expand your depot and get bonuses to better supply your troops. Scavenge the land to see if you get lucky and get extra supplies, there are many options.

Your success hinges on balancing resources, logistics and strategy. Experiment with different types of transports and experience each campaign in different environments with different unique challenges. Will your supplies for the supply depot arrive with train, ships or air?
about_the_game:
Logistics Battalion puts you in charge of logistics and managing to supply the troops with adequate supplies during 1939-1940. Manage the resources and the requests, make sure to deliver on time and choose between several different transport methods.

Your success hinges on balancing resources, logistics and strategy. Experiment with different types of transports and experience each campaign in different environments with different unique challenges. Will your supplies for the supply depot arrive with train, ships or air?

reviews.review_text: I want to like this. I like the idea and the art style, but I broke the game twice within the first 10 minutes. The first time was when clicking the HQ button to make the first supply request. I had no audio and was looking for a menu button and couldn't find one that overrode the tutorial popup. So I hit escape, which took me out to the base overview, but a section of forest was highlighted with an arrow which was leftover from the HQ screen before I hit escape. Had to force close the game and reopen. The second time was when trying to send the first convoy. I don't know what I did, but the game was wanting me to click a "Send" button that didn't exist, so I just gave up at this point. Also note that the music and volume buttons on the main menu don't do anything - I tried clicking both and had zero sound regardless. Also on the selection screen for choosing what vehicles you want to use, I only saw an "Add" button. There was no delete button. So in order to delete you had to confirm the miscounted added vehicles to be taken out to the previous screen, and only there could you delete vehicles. Again, I want to like this game, but it needs work.
